purpose: Provides a properly calibrated assessment of the yield curve's recession-predictive value, replacing the misleading '7/7' framing with Bayesian posterior probabilities
components: Base rate of recession in any 24-month window (25-30%)
Prior yield curve inversions as evidence (including false positives: 1966, 1998, 2022-23)
Term premium conditioning to separate expectations-driven from contaminated signals
current_assessment: Marginal predictive lift is approximately 1.5-2x base rate after proper Bayesian adjustment. The 2022-23 deep inversion (-108bp) without recession (as of March 2026) has either broken the record (7/8) or implies an operationally useless 3+ year lead time. Signal retains value but is far from near-certainty.
limitations: Small sample size constrains all threshold estimates
MOVE conditioning thresholds derived from ~5 observations, likely overfitted
Cannot distinguish between 'false positive' and 'very long lead time'
Term premium conditioning adds model uncertainty
Does not incorporate global curve synchronization
